name: Dehesa Berciana and Villamanta Loop
name_original: Aldea del Fresno, Villamanta – ruta circular
description: Easy 18.9-mile (30.5 km) road cycling route near Aldea del Fresno, offering scenic views of holm oak groves and the Perales River.
region_intro: Road cycling around Aldea Del Fresno offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, characterized by low-altitude river valleys and varied terrain. The region features a mix of riverside vegetation, low mountains, and holm oak, pine, and poplar groves. Cyclists can explore routes along the Alberche and Perales rivers, providing fresh and humid conditions. The area's natural features and existing infrastructure make it suitable for two-wheeled exploration. Best road cycling routes around Aldea Del Fresno The most popular road cycling route is Dehesa Berciana and Villamanta Loop, an easy 18.9 miles (30.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete, winding through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Aldea del Fresno, a difficult 70.4 miles (113.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, including the challenging Cruz Verde Pass. Local road cyclists also love the Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop from Urb. Calalberche - Rio Manzanares, a 52.9 miles (85.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain near the Alberche River, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Aldea Del Fresno is defined by riverside paths, low mountains, and holm oak forests, with the Vía Verde del Alberche offering a notable cycling asset. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aldea Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aldea Del Fresno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aldea Del Fresno?
A: Aldea Del Fresno offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 different tours available. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy rides to challenging climbs.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Aldea Del Fresno?
A: The routes around Aldea Del Fresno offer a good mix of difficulty levels. You'll find 3 easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride, 22 moderate routes for those looking for a bit more challenge, and 26 difficult routes, including options with significant elevation gain like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40644154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Aldea del Fresno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several options suitable for families, particularly the easier routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, while not exclusively for road bikes, offers a mostly favorable and relaxing path through scenic areas, which can be a great option for a family outing. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Aldea Del Fresno?
A: Absolutely. Many of the road cycling routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980239613/dehesa-berciana-and-villamanta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Berciana and Villamanta Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31916987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop from Urb. Calalberche - Rio Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling here?
A: Road cycling around Aldea Del Fresno offers diverse landscapes. You'll encounter lush riverside vegetation along the Alberche and Perales rivers, expansive low mountains, and beautiful holm oak, pine, and poplar groves. The region is known for its tranquil and scenic settings, transitioning from river valleys to forest trails.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich with natural and historical points of interest. You can cycle past the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar del Arroyo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3022683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamantilla Town Center&lt;/a&gt;. The Puente de la Pedrera, a 300-year-old bridge, also offers a notable viewpoint.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Aldea Del Fresno?
A: Aldea Del Fresno is generally pleasant for road cycling, but spring and autumn offer the most comfortable temperatures. The presence of the Alberche and Perales rivers contributes to freshness in the valleys, making it enjoyable even during warmer periods. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Is the Vía Verde del Alberche suitable for road cycling?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Alberche&lt;/a&gt;, utilizing an old railway line, is a significant cycling asset in the region. While greenways can have varied surfaces, this specific route is highlighted for its enjoyable cycling, particularly the section between San Martín de Valdeiglesias and Aldea del Fresno, which passes through pine forests and along the reservoir's edge. It's generally suitable for road bikes, though some sections might be more comfortable on wider tires.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Aldea Del Fresno?
A: The road cycling routes in Aldea Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquil rural environments with low traffic, and the scenic views, especially along the rivers and through the holm oak forests.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40644154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Aldea del Fresno&lt;/a&gt; features over 2,000 meters of ascent, including the demanding Cruz Verde Pass, making it a popular choice for those seeking a strenuous ride.

Q: Where can I find places to rest or refuel during my ride?
A: Many routes pass through or near small towns and villages in the Sierra Oeste region, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and local shops to refuel. Aldea del Fresno itself offers amenities, and routes often connect to other settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar del Arroyo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3022683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamantilla Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, providing opportunities for breaks.

Q: What are the traffic conditions like on the road cycling routes?
A: The Sierra Oeste region, including Aldea Del Fresno, is known for its quiet and safe rural environments. Many cycling routes emphasize traveling along roads with little traffic, offering a peaceful experience. Some routes even boast up to 99% safe paths without significant vehicle traffic, making for a very enjoyable ride.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic views of reservoirs or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its river valleys. Routes frequently follow the Alberche and Perales rivers, offering fresh and humid conditions. You can enjoy panoramic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, especially along sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, and experience the beauty of the 'Playa del Alberche' recreational area.
tips: Villamanta has many Roman archaeological remains and according to some historians, this population could be the ancient Roman villa of Mantua Carpetanorum.

In the hermitage of Nuestra Señora Virgen del Socorro there is a carving of the Virgin of the same name, in polychrome wood, whose antiquity is unknown. It is located at the exit of the municipality on the road that goes to Aldea del Fresno.
You cannot miss the opportunity to visit the Safari Madrid animal park on the farm called El Rincón, where you can observe the animals in a state of semi-freedom, without cages.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy and is well-suited for cyclists of all fitness levels. It features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it an enjoyable experience for those new to road cycling.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by predominantly well-paved and smooth surfaces, ideal for race bikes. You'll cycle through picturesque encinares (holm oak groves) and cerros (hills), with scenic stretches along the ribera del Río Perales (banks of the Perales River).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.5 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 18 minutes to complete, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy scenic views of the natural landscape, including holm oak groves and hills. The route also passes by the banks of the Perales River. Specific points of interest include the Villamanta Town Square, the Roman Well, and the Hermitage of Our Lady Virgin of Socorro.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Aldea del Fresno, Villamanta circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, both Aldea del Fresno and Villamanta are good starting points. You can typically find parking in or near the town centers of these villages to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route that uses public roads and paths. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's best to keep them on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with road cycling environments if you plan to bring them along.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Dehesa Berciana area?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route or to access the general Dehesa Berciana area it passes through.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region southwest of Madrid is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Aldea del Fresno and Villamanta have amenities like cafes and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Ruta de Berciana and the Ferrocarril abandonado Madrid Almorox.

faqs.question: Is there a particularly challenging section on this route?
faqs.answer: This specific circular route is generally easy. However, some related routes in the wider area mention a potential "long climb" after crossing the Perales River. On this particular route, any climbs are considered manageable for an easy classification.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady Virgin of Socorro
